LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT CYVERA INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D AND THE MATTER IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 1ST, 3RD, 4TH, 5TH, 6TH & 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR THE REASON STATED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING AND AMPLIFIED AT THE HEARING. THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND UPON PLAINTIFF RECEIVING THE ORDER RE: THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND ONLY TO ALLEGE A CLAIM THAT CYVERA NEGLIGENTLY MISREPRESENTED ITS CURRENT STATE OF PARTICIPATION IN DISCUSSIONS ABOUT BEING ACQUIRED. THERE MAY NOT BE ANY FURTHER DEMURRERS FILED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION WHERE THE DEMURRER HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN OVERRULED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHELLE G. LEE & ATTY. DAVID A. LOWE OF THE RUDY EXELROD & LOWE LLP, TEL. NO. 814-6551 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BENJAMIN EMMET OF LITTLER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (408) 795-3496 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT CYVERA INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is taken under submission as to the 7th cause of action. The demurrer is overruled as to the 1st, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 6th & 8th causes of action for the reason stated in the tentative ruling and amplified at the hearing. The demurrer to the second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is sustained with 10 days leave to amend upon Plaintiff receiving the order re: the 7th cause of action. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend only to allege a claim that Cyvera negligently misrepresented its current state of participation in discussions about being acquired. There may not be any further demurrers filed as to causes of action where the demurrer has previously been overruled. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Continued To Dec-31-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. NICHELLE G. LEE & ATTY. DAVID A. LOWE OF THE RUDY EXELROD & LOWE LLP, TEL. NO. 814-6551 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BENJAMIN EMMERT, TEL. NO. (408) 998-4150 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PALO ALTO NETWORKS, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled on the grounds that the Complaint fails to allege a viable basis for liability. The Complaint explicitly alleges that "following the acquisition, there was a substantial continuity of Cyvera's business operations, including but not limited to the use of the same employees, same locations, substantially same supervisory personnel, sale of substantially the same products and substantially same working conditions as prior to the sale." (Compl. 9.) Thus, Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged facts that support the imposition of successor liability. (See Cleveland v. Johnson (2012) 209 Cal.App. 4th 1315, 1334 ["If a corporation organizes another corporation with practically the same shareholders and directors, transfers all the assets but does not pay all the first corporation's debts, and continues to carry on the same business, the separate entities may be disregarded and the new corporation held liable for the obligations of the old."].) Next the Demurrer is sustained as to the cause of action for fraud, with 10 days leave to amend to allow Plaintiff to specifically allege who made the misrepresentations. The Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for negligent misrepresentation, fraudulent misrepresentation under California Labor Code section 970, breach of contract, and breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. The Complaint sufficiently alleges the requisite elements of each claim. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Plaintiff must allege the violation of a fundamental public policy, evidenced or tethered to some statutory or constitutional provision, that supports his claim. (Cf. Gould v. Maryland Sound Industries, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1137, 1148 [holding that prompt payment of earned and unpaid wages was a fundamental public policy evidenced by multiple statutes].) The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the cause of action for promissory estoppel for Plaintiff to allege, if he can and in the alternative, facts supporting the lack of consideration of the employment contract. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the claim for unfair business practices in violation of Business and Professions Code section 17200. Plaintiff is required to plead facts establishing a cognizable claim to the "Commencement Option shares," rather than pleading a legal conclusion of ownership. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
